title: Top European Dividend Stocks To Consider In April 2026
content:
Despite recent downturns in the European markets, with the STOXX Europe 600 Index and major indices like Germany's DAX and France's CAC 40 experiencing declines, traditionally defensive sectors such as utilities and telecoms have shown resilience amid heightened geopolitical risks. In this environment, dividend stocks can offer stability by providing regular income streams to investors, making them an attractive consideration for those looking to navigate uncertain market conditions.

Top 10 Dividend Stocks In Europe

Name Dividend Yield Dividend Rating Zurich Insurance Group (SWX:ZURN) 4.56% ★★★★★★ Zinzino (OM:ZZ B) 4.72% ★★★★★★ Teleperformance (ENXTPA:TEP) 8.16% ★★★★★★ Telekom Austria (WBAG:TKA) 4.42% ★★★★★★ Swiss Re (SWX:SREN) 5.09% ★★★★★★ Rubis (ENXTPA:RUI) 5.97% ★★★★★★ Hannover Rück (XTRA:HNR1) 4.88% ★★★★★★ DKSH Holding (SWX:DKSH) 4.27% ★★★★★★ Bucher Industries (SWX:BUCN) 3.59% ★★★★★★ Banque Cantonale Vaudoise (SWX:BCVN) 3.59% ★★★★★★

Click here to see the full list of 213 stocks from our Top European Dividend Stocks screener.

Let's review some notable picks from our screened stocks.

Amundi

Simply Wall St Dividend Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Amundi S.A. is a publicly owned investment manager with a market cap of approximately €16.62 billion.

Operations: Amundi S.A. generates its revenue through various investment management services and financial products offered to clients.

Dividend Yield: 5.2%

Amundi offers a mixed dividend profile, with a payout ratio of 54.9% indicating dividends are covered by earnings and cash flows. However, its dividend history has been volatile over the past decade. Despite this, the company announced an annual dividend of €4.25 per share for 2026. Amundi's price-to-earnings ratio (10.4x) suggests it trades at good value compared to the French market average (16.1x). Recent strategic moves include a €500 million share buyback program and expansion in Asia through key appointments.

Get an in-depth perspective on Amundi's performance by reading our dividend report here. Our comprehensive valuation report raises the possibility that Amundi is priced lower than what may be justified by its financials.ENXTPA:AMUN Dividend History as at Apr 2026

Atea

Simply Wall St Dividend Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: Atea ASA offers IT infrastructure and related solutions to businesses and public sector organizations across the Nordic countries and Baltic regions, with a market cap of NOK17.16 billion.

Operations: Atea ASA generates revenue through its IT infrastructure and solutions provided to both businesses and public sector entities in the Nordic and Baltic regions.

Dividend Yield: 4.9%

Atea ASA pays a reliable 4.86% dividend, supported by its cash flow and earnings with payout ratios of 69.6% and 75.2%, respectively. Despite being lower than Norway's top dividend payers, Atea's dividends have grown steadily over the past decade. Recent Q1 results show robust growth with net income rising to NOK 389 million from NOK 162 million year-over-year, reinforcing its capacity to sustain dividends, which increased to NOK 7.50 per share for 2026 in two installments.

Story Continues

Take a closer look at Atea's potential here in our dividend report. Upon reviewing our latest valuation report, Atea's share price might be too pessimistic.OB:ATEA Dividend History as at Apr 2026

Strabag

Simply Wall St Dividend Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: Strabag SE is involved in construction projects and has a market cap of €10.18 billion.

Operations: Strabag SE generates revenue through its construction projects.

Dividend Yield: 3.3%

Strabag's dividend payments have been volatile over the past decade, with a low yield of 3.29% compared to Austria's top payers. Despite this, dividends are well covered by earnings and cash flow with payout ratios of 36.5% and 29.4%, respectively. Recent financial results show an increase in net income to €916.28 million for 2025 from €823 million in the previous year, indicating potential stability in future payouts amidst ongoing revenue growth projects like Slovenia’s infrastructure development contract valued at €194 million.

Dive into the specifics of Strabag here with our thorough dividend report. Our valuation report unveils the possibility Strabag's shares may be trading at a discount.WBAG:STR Dividend History as at Apr 2026

title: Amundi Leadership Changes Put Focus On Operations And Asian Client Growth
content:
Never miss an important update on your stock portfolio and cut through the noise. Over 7 million investors trust Simply Wall St to stay informed where it matters for FREE.

Amundi (ENXTPA:AMUN) has promoted a new Chief Operating Officer, signaling a leadership reshuffle at the group level. The firm has also hired a former Citigroup banker as head of corporate clients for Asia ex-China, focusing on regional institutional relationships. These appointments highlight a focus on operational execution and on building the corporate client franchise across Asian markets outside mainland China.

As one of Europe’s largest asset managers, Amundi operates across active and passive strategies, multi asset products and solutions for retail and institutional clients. The latest leadership moves come as global asset managers continue to refine their operating models and client coverage in response to regulatory change, fee pressure and growing demand for cross border investment capabilities.

For investors tracking ENXTPA:AMUN, the new COO and Asia ex-China corporate client head may influence how resources are allocated across regions and client segments. The impact of these decisions will depend on how effectively Amundi focuses on operational priorities and builds deeper relationships with corporate clients in key Asian markets over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Amundi by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Amundi.ENXTPA:AMUN 1-Year Stock Price Chart

Does the team leading Amundi have what it takes? See our full breakdown of the management team's track record and compensation.

Quick Assessment

✅ Price vs Analyst Target: At €74.1, Amundi trades about 10% below the €82.3 analyst target. ⚖️ Simply Wall St Valuation: Shares are described as trading close to estimated fair value. ❌ Recent Momentum: The 30 day return is about a 2% decline.

Timing decisions such as buying, selling or holding Amundi depend on individual circumstances and further research. For more detail, see Simply Wall St's company report for the latest analysis of Amundi's fair value.

Key Considerations

📊 The new COO and Asia ex China corporate client head could influence how efficiently Amundi runs its operations and serves institutional clients in key markets. 📊 It may be useful to monitor whether assets under management, revenue mix by region and corporate client wins in Asia ex China change following these appointments. ⚠️ Forecast earnings are described as expected to decline by an average of 1.5% per year over the next 3 years, so execution by the refreshed leadership team is an important factor to watch.

Story Continues

title: Amundi: Publication of the 2025 Universal Registration Document
content:
Amundi

Amundi: Publication of the 2025 Universal Registration Document

Amundi announces the filing of its 2025 Universal Registration Document to the French securities regulator, the Autorité des Marchés Financiers (AMF), on 31 March 2025, under the registration number D.26-0183.

This 2025 Universal Registration Document is now available in French only on the AMF website (www.amf-france.org) and in the shareholders section of the Amundi website (http://about.amundi.com), in both French and English. It is also available at Amundi Head Office at 91–93 Boulevard Pasteur, 75015 Paris, or by request through mail at investor.relations@amundi.com.

The following documents are included in the 2025 Universal Registration Document:

the annual financial report; the sustainability statement; the report on corporate governance; information relating to the fees of statutory auditors.

About Amundi

Amundi, the leading European asset manager, ranking among the top 10 global players1, offers its 200 million clients - retail, institutional and corporate - a complete range of savings and investment solutions in active and passive management, in traditional or real assets. This offering is enhanced with IT tools and services to cover the entire savings value chain. A subsidiary of the Crédit Agricole group and listed on the stock exchange, Amundi currently manages close to €2.4 trillion of assets2.

With its six international investment hubs3, financial and extra-financial research capabilities and long-standing commitment to responsible investment, Amundi is a key player in the asset management landscape.

Amundi clients benefit from the expertise and advice of 5,400 employees in 34 countries.

Amundi, a trusted partner, working every day in the interest of its clients and society

www.amundi.com

title: Investors Game Out Stagflation Risk in Japan as Oil Prices Rise
content:
(Bloomberg) -- Some investors are starting to position for a scenario Japan hasn’t faced in decades, as rising oil prices and a weaker currency stoke stagflation worries and force a rethink of bets.

Global funds including Allianz Global Investors and Amundi SA are already hedging against stagflation — a mix of rising prices and slowing growth — with the Iran conflict nearing the one-month mark. Allianz has reinforced its underweight view on Japanese government bonds and turned neutral on equities and the yen while using options to hedge volatility. Amundi has trimmed its overweight stance for Japanese stocks and is neutral on the nation’s currency, with intervention risks in mind.

While stagflation isn’t the central scenario for most investors in Japan, the risk carries a lot of weight for an economy that only recently emerged from decades of deflation. A slide into stagflation would challenge the country’s recovery narrative and broader investment case. Wages are rising, but investors warn that a prolonged period of elevated oil prices could erode those gains before they translate into stronger spending.

Japan is feeling the pain of the war acutely as it imports more than 90% of its crude from the Middle East and expensive oil is seen transmitting pricing pressures into the economy and squeezing household incomes. It’s a toxic condition the country hasn’t faced since the oil shock of the 1970s, and if crude prices remain elevated for a prolonged period, it is seen hurting consumer demand and economic activity.

That leaves the Bank of Japan facing a difficult trade-off: tighten policy to support the yen and contain inflation expectations, or hold back to avoid undermining growth that helped stocks to fresh record highs last month. It also risks exacerbating fiscal concerns, which are already elevated after Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s election victory, as a stagflation shock could prompt additional government spending.

“Higher oil prices reinforce the perception that the BOJ is behind the curve, which can be a continued source of yen weakness in the near term,” said Florian Neto, Amundi’s head of investment in Asia. “The main impact is that if the yen depreciates further, it erodes purchasing power for Japanese households, which are already struggling with elevated living costs. That creates a negative feedback loop” that could eventually weigh on corporate earnings, Neto added.

Bond Curve Flattener

Some investors are positioning for a flattening of Japan’s yield curve, a trade that typically benefits when shorter-term yields rise on expectations of policy tightening while longer-term yields are capped by growth concerns.

Story Continues

Kensuke Niihara, State Street Investment Management’s chief investment officer for Japan, said curve flattener trades, which favor longer-dated bonds over shorter maturity debt, could perform better in the current environment. He cited rising inflation risks alongside a weaker growth outlook for his preference. Amundi’s Neto also expects a flattening in the near-term, before returning to steepening over the medium term on fiscal concerns.

But asset managers like Stefan Rittner, a senior portfolio manager at Allianz Global Investors, have turned more cautious on duration overall. With inflation risks tilted sharply to the upside and the government extending energy subsidies, the technical and fundamental backdrop for JGBs has deteriorated, he said.

Weak Currency

Japan’s dependence on imported oil means the yen is more sensitive to inflationary pressures and less effective as a safe haven currency in times of volatility. Hedge funds have boosted their short yen bets to the most in two months, maintaining pressure on the Japanese currency.

While some investors see the yen as undervalued, near-term risks point toward further weakness in the currency if oil prices stay elevated or if the BOJ’s policy normalization proceeds slowly.

Equity Rotation

In equities, investors are focusing on resilience and pricing power. Global exporters and companies tied to structural growth themes — such as semiconductor equipment — are seen in a better position to navigate higher costs and currency volatility.

“The impact of a stagflationary environment in Japan would largely be determined by a company’s ability to manage input cost pressures and sustain pricing power,” said Daniel Hurley, T. Rowe Price Group Inc.’s portfolio specialist for global equities and Japanese equities strategies. Hurley said he is closely monitoring such risks even as he is yet to alter his broader conviction on Japan.

By contrast, domestic-demand driven sectors are more exposed. Areas such as consumer discretionary, transportation and utilities face pressure if entities are unable to pass on the higher input costs.

Yet many investors are more optimistic. “At this stage, stagflation in Japan looks like a tail risk rather than a base case,” said Ivy Ng, APAC Chief Investment Officer at DWS. “The outlook hinges on two variables with high uncertainty: how high oil prices ultimately peak and how long any disruption around the Strait of Hormuz persists.”

Still, the shift in positioning shows how quickly Japan’s recovery narrative can be challenged, with the fine balance between economic growth and inflation remaining a key challenge for monetary officials.

“Markets may still be underestimating the BOJ’s dilemma,” said Allianz Global Investors’ Rittner. “This narrow path makes ‘policy error’ a much more credible risk than it was six months ago.”

--With assistance from Alice French.

More stories like this are available on bloomberg.com

©2026 Bloomberg L.P.

title: Why The Narrative Around Amundi (ENXTPA:AMUN) Is Shifting After Fresh Valuation And Earnings Calls
content:
Never miss an important update on your stock portfolio and cut through the noise. Over 7 million investors trust Simply Wall St to stay informed where it matters for FREE.

What Has Changed In Amundi’s Valuation Setup?

Analysts have refreshed their models on Amundi, with the fair value estimate moving from about €78.03 to roughly €81.10, anchored by a lower discount rate that lifts the present value of expected cash flows. At the same time, revenue assumptions have shifted from a 19.47% decline to positive growth of about 2.09%, which helps explain why the valuation anchor now sits closer to the upper end of recent target ranges. As you read on, you will see how this evolving narrative is taking shape and how you can keep on top of future updates as they come through.

Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story. Head over to our Company Report to find new ways to value Amundi.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

Citi, RBC Capital, JPMorgan and Morgan Stanley have all issued price targets between about €71 and €82.10, which frames much of the recent discussion around where Amundi's valuation could sit relative to current expectations. Citi has repeatedly lifted its target on Amundi, most recently to €78.10 from €73, while keeping a Buy rating. This signals that the firm has been comfortable with how management executes and how the business supports its cash flow outlook. RBC Capital moved its target to €71 from €66 and kept a Sector Perform rating, and JPMorgan revised its target to €75 from €71 with a Neutral rating. This suggests that even more cautious firms still see room for the shares to align with their revised assumptions on costs, revenues and balance sheet discipline. Morgan Stanley set one of the higher targets at €82.10 from €70.80 and reaffirmed an Equal Weight rating, tying its view to how Amundi maintains execution quality while managing near term risks that could influence earnings and flows. Across these reports, analysts tend to reward Amundi for execution and cost control, while flagging valuation, the possibility that a lot of upside is already priced in and shorter term market risks as the main watchpoints.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

The tone is not uniformly positive, with Citi most recently downgrading Amundi. This shows that at least one previously bullish house is now more cautious on the risk and reward trade off at current levels. The presence of Sector Perform and Neutral ratings from RBC Capital and JPMorgan indicates that some analysts see the shares as fairly valued against their models, with limited clear upside without stronger evidence on growth or margin trends. Across the more cautious views, the key reservations cluster around valuation, the idea that a meaningful portion of potential upside may already be priced in and near term risks that could affect Amundi's ability to deliver on the cash flow profile underpinning these targets.

Story Continues

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives or begin writing your own Narrative!ENXTPA:AMUN 1-Year Stock Price Chart

What's in the News

Amundi launched a share repurchase program of up to €500 million, with the stated intention to cancel the repurchased shares and reduce share capital, running no later than January 26, 2027. The Board of Directors authorized a new buyback plan on February 3, 2026, adding another capital return tool alongside the existing €500 million program. A Board meeting on February 2, 2026, was scheduled to review and approve the fourth quarter and full year 2025 financial statements, a notable event for investors tracking earnings and balance sheet developments. Amundi and ICG announced a long term partnership in which Amundi will distribute ICG products in the wealth channel, jointly develop new wealth focused products, and acquire a 9.9% economic stake in ICG, together with a financial target for 2028 that sets a goal of earnings per share above €7.

How This Changes the Fair Value For Amundi

Fair Value: Updated analyst fair value has risen from about €78.03 to roughly €81.10, which is a modest uplift in the valuation anchor you might use as a reference point. Discount Rate: The discount rate has moved from about 10.89% to around 10.46%. A lower rate in the model increases the present value of projected cash flows and helps explain part of the higher fair value output. Revenue Growth: The revenue growth assumption has shifted from a 19.47% decline to positive growth of about 2.09%. That is a meaningful reset in how future € revenue is modeled and feeds directly into the updated cash flow profile. Profit Margin: The profit margin input has gone from roughly 40.66% to about 42.35%, implying a larger share of € revenue flowing through to earnings in the framework analysts are using. Future P/E: The future P/E multiple has been trimmed slightly from around 15.09x to about 14.77x, which points to a more cautious stance on how much investors might pay for those modeled future earnings, even though the fair value output has moved higher.

🔔 Never Miss an Update: Follow The Narrative

Narratives on Simply Wall St let you connect Amundi's story to the numbers by setting out your view on its future revenue, earnings and margins, then linking that to a fair value. They sit on the Community page, update automatically when fresh news or earnings arrive, and help you compare Fair Value to the current price so you can decide whether the stock looks attractive or stretched at any moment.

If you want to see how all of this comes together for Amundi, start with the original community view on the company.

How growth in European ETFs, responsible investments and technology services fits into Amundi's revenue and earnings outlook in the current consensus view. Why analysts link Amundi's partnerships in Asia and private markets deals to assumptions about profit margins and a future P/E of about 15x. What risks around French tax proposals, asset outflows and competition could challenge the case behind the current fair value and price targets.

Head over to the Simply Wall St Community and follow the Amundi Narrative at this link so you can see how the story, forecasts and fair value evolve in one place. Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

title: How New Price Targets Are Reframing The Story For Amundi (ENXTPA:AMUN)
content:
Get insights on thousands of stocks from the global community of over 7 million individual investors at Simply Wall St.

Amundi’s latest price target update has shifted the conversation around the stock, putting fresh focus on how the market is currently framing its prospects. With the target now revised from a previously unspecified level, investors are reassessing what this means for expectations and sentiment. Stay with this article to see how you can keep on top of these narrative shifts as they unfold.

Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story. Head over to our Company Report to find new ways to value Amundi.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives or begin writing your own Narrative!ENXTPA:AMUN 1-Year Stock Price Chart

How This Changes the Fair Value For Amundi

The updated price target gives you a refreshed anchor for what some analysts consider a reasonable fair value range for Amundi, based on their current assumptions. Changes in a price target do not automatically mean the underlying business has changed; they mostly reflect how the analyst is interpreting existing information at this point in time. You can treat the new target as one reference point alongside your own work on earnings, cash flows, P/E or P/B multiples, rather than a stand-alone verdict on what the shares should trade at. Comparing the current market price with the latest target can help you see how much optimism or caution is already reflected in Amundi’s share price, without relying on a single fair value number. If you track future revisions to this target and others, you can build a picture of how sentiment around Amundi’s fair value evolves over time, which may be useful context when you review your own thesis.

title: European Dividend Stocks To Enhance Your Portfolio
content:
As the European markets continue to show optimism with indices like the STOXX Europe 600 Index ending higher amid a strengthening eurozone economy, investors are increasingly drawn to dividend stocks as a means of enhancing portfolio stability. In this context, selecting stocks that offer reliable dividends can be particularly appealing, providing both income and potential growth in an environment characterized by favorable interest rates and improving industrial production.

Top 10 Dividend Stocks In Europe

Name Dividend Yield Dividend Rating Zurich Insurance Group (SWX:ZURN) 4.30% ★★★★★★ Telekom Austria (WBAG:TKA) 4.56% ★★★★★★ Les Docks des Pétroles d'Ambès -SA (ENXTPA:DPAM) 5.76% ★★★★★★ Holcim (SWX:HOLN) 3.92% ★★★★★★ HEXPOL (OM:HPOL B) 4.88% ★★★★★★ Evolution (OM:EVO) 4.95% ★★★★★★ DKSH Holding (SWX:DKSH) 3.95% ★★★★★★ Credito Emiliano (BIT:CE) 4.78% ★★★★★☆ Cembra Money Bank (SWX:CMBN) 4.25% ★★★★★★ Bravida Holding (OM:BRAV) 4.15% ★★★★★★

We'll examine a selection from our screener results.

Piquadro

Simply Wall St Dividend Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: Piquadro S.p.A. designs, produces, and markets leather goods and accessories in Italy, Europe, and worldwide with a market cap of €122.99 million.

Operations: Piquadro S.p.A. generates its revenue from three main segments: Lancel (€72.96 million), Piquadro (€76.92 million), and The Bridge (€37.63 million).

Dividend Yield: 5.7%

Piquadro's dividend payments are well-covered by both earnings and cash flows, with payout ratios of 59.7% and 46.6%, respectively. Despite a history of volatility, dividends have grown over the past decade. The stock trades at a discount to its estimated fair value, offering potential value for investors seeking income. However, the unstable track record may concern those prioritizing consistent dividend reliability. Recent earnings show modest growth in revenue and net income, reflecting steady financial performance.

Get an in-depth perspective on Piquadro's performance by reading our dividend report here. Our expertly prepared valuation report Piquadro implies its share price may be lower than expected.BIT:PQ Dividend History as at Jan 2026

Amundi

Simply Wall St Dividend Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: Amundi S.A. is a publicly owned investment manager with a market capitalization of approximately €15.24 billion, focusing on providing asset management services globally.

Operations: Amundi S.A.'s revenue is primarily derived from its Asset Management segment, which generated approximately €6.77 billion.

Dividend Yield: 5.7%

Amundi's dividend is well-supported by earnings and cash flows, with payout ratios of 52.3% and 47.6%, respectively. Despite being among the top dividend payers in France, its dividends have been volatile over the past decade. The stock trades at a favorable price-to-earnings ratio of 9.6x compared to the French market average of 16.6x, presenting potential value for investors seeking income amid an unstable dividend history and forecasted earnings decline. Recent partnerships could enhance distribution capabilities and product offerings in private markets.

Story Continues

Take a closer look at Amundi's potential here in our dividend report. The analysis detailed in our Amundi valuation report hints at an deflated share price compared to its estimated value.ENXTPA:AMUN Dividend History as at Jan 2026

AB SKF

Simply Wall St Dividend Rating: ★★★★☆☆

Overview: AB SKF (OM:SKF B) is a global company that designs, manufactures, and sells bearings and units, seals, lubrication systems, condition monitoring, and related services with a market capitalization of approximately SEK116.19 billion.

Operations: AB SKF generates revenue through two main segments: Automotive, contributing SEK27.16 billion, and Industrial, accounting for SEK67.18 billion.

Dividend Yield: 3%

AB SKF's dividend payments have been volatile over the past decade, despite recent growth. The 3.04% yield is below Sweden's top tier but remains covered by earnings and cash flows with payout ratios around 72%. Recent inclusion in the OMX Nordic 40 Index and innovations in sustainable technologies highlight SKF's commitment to long-term growth. However, recent financials show a decline in sales and net income, which could impact future dividend stability.

title: How Investors May Respond To Amundi (ENXTPA:AMUN) Promoting AI-Aware Portfolio Diversification Strategies
content:
In recent days, Amundi has urged investors to prioritise broader portfolio diversification as the AI investment cycle expands and influences asset allocation decisions. This emphasis on spreading risk across sectors and themes highlights how Amundi is aligning its asset management approach with evolving technology-related trends. We’ll now examine how Amundi’s push for AI-aware diversification interacts with its existing investment narrative and long-term earnings assumptions.

Find companies with promising cash flow potential yet trading below their fair value.

Amundi Investment Narrative Recap

To own Amundi, you need to believe in its ability to grow fee-based revenues across ETFs, technology services and partnerships, even as markets shift and regulation tightens. The recent call for AI-aware diversification supports this multi-pillar story but does not materially change the near term focus on managing tax headwinds and client mandate concentration risks.

The most connected development is Amundi’s 2028 financial target of EPS above €7, which frames how AI-related themes might be integrated into its growth drivers, including ETFs, Technology & Services and private markets partnerships. Together with the ICG distribution agreement, this reinforces the importance of broad product depth if investor interest rotates quickly between AI-linked and traditional strategies.

Yet, against this constructive backdrop, investors should be aware that the proposed exceptional French tax contribution could...

Read the full narrative on Amundi (it's free!)

Amundi's narrative projects €3.7 billion revenue and €1.5 billion earnings by 2028. This implies revenues shrinking by 18.4% per year and an earnings decrease of €0.2 billion from €1.7 billion today.

Uncover how Amundi's forecasts yield a €74.47 fair value, in line with its current price.

Exploring Other PerspectivesENXTPA:AMUN 1-Year Stock Price Chart

Seven fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community span roughly €44 to about €142, underlining how far opinions can diverge. Against that backdrop, Amundi’s focus on broad AI-aware diversification puts the spotlight on how resilient its fee base may be under different market and policy outcomes, so you may want to compare several of these views before deciding how this fits your portfolio.

title: Assessing Amundi (ENXTPA:AMUN)’s Valuation After Its Recent Steady Share Price Climb
content:
Amundi (ENXTPA:AMUN) has quietly moved higher over the past month, adding roughly 2% and extending gains to about 12% over the past year, even as annual revenue and profit growth remain negative.

See our latest analysis for Amundi.

That steady 90 day share price return of 8.41 percent, backed by a 1 year total shareholder return of 11.8 percent and an impressive 3 year total shareholder return of nearly 54 percent, suggests sentiment is gradually improving around Amundi’s earnings power and risk profile, even if the latest closing share price of $68.3 is not flashing obvious bargain territory.

If Amundi’s measured climb has you thinking about where else capital is compounding, this could be a good moment to explore fast growing stocks with high insider ownership for other ideas with strong alignment between management and shareholders.

With the shares now trading only modestly below analyst targets despite shrinking revenue and earnings, the key question is whether Amundi is quietly undervalued or if the market is already pricing in a rebound in growth.

Most Popular Narrative: 7.5% Undervalued

With Amundi last closing at €68.3 against a narrative fair value of €73.8, the current price implies room for upside if the story holds.

The expansion in Technology & Services, with technology revenues up by 42%, highlights anticipated earnings growth as Amundi diversifies and increases its client base across geographies.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious how shrinking top line expectations can still justify a higher value per share? The crux is margins, mix, and a future earnings multiple that assumes resilience, not hypergrowth. Want to see exactly how those moving parts build to that target fair value and what has to go right for it to stick?

Result: Fair Value of €73.8 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, that upside case could be knocked off course if the new French exceptional tax bites harder than expected or Asian joint ventures disappoint.

Find out about the key risks to this Amundi narrative.

Another Angle on Value

Our SWS DCF model paints a cooler picture, suggesting Amundi is actually overvalued at about €68.3 versus an estimated fair value near €62.4. If cash flows do not grow as hoped, could today’s seemingly reasonable price leave less margin for error than it appears?

title: Assessing Amundi (ENXTPA:AMUN) Valuation: Is the Current Share Price Hiding Long-Term Opportunity?
content:
Amundi (ENXTPA:AMUN) shares have seen modest movement over the past month, catching the eye of investors interested in the dynamics of Europe’s asset management sector. The company’s recent returns provide some perspective on market sentiment.

See our latest analysis for Amundi.

While Amundi’s 1-year share price return has been muted, its 1-year total shareholder return of 4.7% tells a fuller story when you factor in dividends. Steady three-year and five-year total returns of nearly 48% and 33% respectively show investors have been rewarded for their patience, even as recent price momentum looks modest.

If you’re curious about what else markets might be rewarding this year, now is an ideal time to broaden your search and discover fast growing stocks with high insider ownership

The key question now is whether Amundi’s current share price offers hidden value, or if investors are already factoring in all the growth ahead. Is there an opportunity for buyers, or is everything already reflected in the price?

Most Popular Narrative: 9% Undervalued

With the most popular narrative setting Amundi's fair value at €73.21, shares look attractively priced versus the recent close at €66.60. What makes this estimate notable is not just the discount but also the forward focus of its detailed assumptions. This invites scrutiny into whether near-term caution matches longer-term potential.

The partnership with SBI in India and a direct presence in other Asian markets underscore an expected rise in revenues from increased market penetration and capturing growing client assets in these regions.

Read the complete narrative.
